fixture.description: Local OpenFootball fixture context already bundled in the tournament KG.

deep_fixture.description: Deterministic local fixture stress-test source for graph-shape tests.

existing_kg.description: Reuse the selected tournament KG as context input for the local scouting run.

polymarket_api.description: Backward-compatible alias for Polymarket Gamma market discovery.

public.description: Existing public-data scout for web/news/search-derived scouting claims.

public_x.description: Public scout plus ScrapeCreators Google Search filtered to X/Twitter for low-confidence social, injury, and lineup leads.
public_x.setup_hint: Create a ScrapeCreators API key, then set SCRAPECREATORS_API_KEY and SCRAPECREATORS_X_SEARCH_URL in colony/.env. Recommended URL: https://api.scrapecreators.com/v1/google/search?query={query}.

public_camel.description: Public scout with deeper CAMEL-style/DDGS research queries enabled.

camel_deep_research.description: Deep scouting orchestrator using CAMEL native structured agents when enabled, with role-scoped DDGS fallback and local claim critic.

wikidata_profiles.description: No-key Wikidata SPARQL profile resolver for match teams and linked football players.

query.query: PREFIX wd: <http://www.wikidata.org/entity/> PREFIX wdt: <http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/> PREFIX rdfs: <http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#> PREFIX schema: <http://schema.org/> PREFIX wikibase: <http://wikiba.se/ontology#> PREFIX bd: <http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#> SELECT ?kind ?team ?teamLabel ?teamDescription ?country ?countryLabel ?coach ?coachLabel ?player ?playerLabel ?playerDescription ?position ?positionLabel ?club ?clubLabel WHERE { VALUES ?wantedTeamLabel { "{team1} national football team"@en "{team2} national football team"@en } ?team rdfs:label ?wantedTeamLabel. { BIND("team" AS ?kind) OPTIONAL { ?team schema:description ?teamDescription FILTER(LANG(?teamDescription) = "en") } OPTIONAL { ?team wdt:P1532 ?country. } OPTIONAL { ?team wdt:P286 ?coach. } } UNION { BIND("player" AS ?kind) ?player wdt:P54 ?team. ?player wdt:P106/wdt:P279* wd:Q937857. FILTER NOT EXISTS { ?player wdt:P570 ?deathDate. } OPTIONAL { ?player schema:description ?playerDescription FILTER(LANG(?playerDescription) = "en") } OPTIONAL { ?player wdt:P413 ?position. } OPTIONAL { ?player wdt:P54 ?club. FILTER(?club != ?team) } } SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language "en". } } LIMIT 80

txline_fixture.description: TXLINE World Cup fixture snapshot, used to resolve provider fixture IDs.

txline_scores.description: TXLINE score/status snapshot after txline_fixture resolves the fixture ID.

txline_odds_historical.description: TXLINE odds snapshot before kickoff, after txline_fixture resolves the fixture ID and timestamp.

txline_full.description: Convenience bundle for TXLINE fixture, score/status, and historical odds snapshots.
txline_full.setup_hint: Activate the World Cup free tier, then set TXLINE_JWT and TXLINE_API_TOKEN in Railway Variables.

polymarket_gamma.description: Polymarket Gamma public-search resolver for validated football match markets/events.

polymarket_clob.description: Polymarket Gamma discovery enriched with public CLOB midpoint/orderbook for validated football markets.

polymarket_market_context.description: Convenience bundle for Polymarket market context using Gamma discovery plus CLOB price/orderbook enrichment.

datasources.name: OpenFootball World Cup
datasources.coverage: World Cup schedules, groups, venues, historical tournament files.
datasources.notes: Good base layer; not enough for live scouting or player-level KG.

datasources.name: TXLINE World Cup API
datasources.auth: guest JWT plus activated read-only API token
datasources.coverage: World Cup fixtures, odds, score/status rows, historical replay, player/stat endpoints per docs.
datasources.notes: Best live match backbone we have tested. Keep wallet signing outside KG/scouting manifests.

datasources.name: Polymarket Gamma/CLOB APIs
datasources.auth: none for public Gamma/search; auth only for trading endpoints, which scouting must not use
datasources.coverage: Gamma market/event discovery plus public CLOB midpoint, bid/ask spread, orderbook depth, liquidity and market price context.
datasources.notes: Useful market sentiment/liquidity source. Gamma resolves valid football markets; CLOB enriches token outcomes with midpoint, spread, and depth. Keep this as market_context, not player/tactical scouting.

datasources.name: Existing KG enrichment
datasources.coverage: Any prior KG entities, relationships, findings, and evidence claims.
datasources.notes: Lets later runs reuse older graph context without rerunning every scout.

datasources.name: Public web/news/URL sources
datasources.coverage: Team news, squads, injuries/returns, lineups, recent form, coach form, player ratings, attacking/defensive profiles, tactical notes.
datasources.notes: High coverage but noisy; should prefer official federation, FIFA, league, or reputable news pages.

datasources.name: CAMEL deep research agents
datasources.auth: optional LLM key for native CAMEL; no key required for DDGS fallback
datasources.coverage: Role-specialized deep scouting for injuries, predicted lineups, tactical matchup, recent form, player ratings, coach form, rosters, match history, and social-signal candidates.
datasources.notes: Use for deep mode or KG gaps. Native mode requires COLONY_CAMEL_USE_NATIVE=1 plus an LLM key; every accepted claim is source-grounded and passes the local critic before entering the KG.

datasources.name: ScrapeCreators X Search API
datasources.auth: ScrapeCreators API key plus a valid X/Twitter search endpoint URL.
datasources.coverage: Real-time posts, sentiment, injury rumors, lineup leaks, training notes, journalist/team chatter.
datasources.notes: Dashboard plugin label: ScrapeCreators X search. Set SCRAPECREATORS_API_KEY and SCRAPECREATORS_X_SEARCH_URL in colony/.env; the recommended endpoint is https://api.scrapecreators.com/v1/google/search?query={query}. Do not treat social posts as factual claims unless corroborated; keep them as low-confidence social signals or availability leads.

datasources.name: The Odds API
datasources.coverage: Upcoming/live events, bookmaker odds, scores, historical odds and event odds depending plan.
datasources.notes: Good multi-bookmaker odds aggregation candidate. Payload is nested by event/bookmaker/market/outcome, so a small adapter is cleaner than row_claims.

datasources.name: Betfair Exchange API
datasources.auth: Betfair developer app key and account auth
datasources.coverage: Exchange market catalogues, back/lay prices, liquidity, in-play exchange movement.
datasources.notes: Useful for sharper exchange pricing/liquidity. Needs account/API access and responsible gambling/geography checks.

datasources.name: Pinnacle API
datasources.coverage: Sportsbook lines, prices, limits, market movement depending access.
datasources.notes: Potential sharp-book reference source, but access and regional restrictions need verification before use.

datasources.name: OddsJam API
datasources.coverage: Sports odds aggregation, line movement, bookmaker comparison, arbitrage-style metadata depending plan.
datasources.notes: Good candidate if we want market consensus across many books without scraping individual betting sites.

datasources.name: Wikidata Query Service
datasources.coverage: Entities, teams, players, clubs, countries, identifiers, biographical context.
datasources.notes: Good entity-resolution layer; not a live squad, injury, or lineup source.

datasources.name: StatsBomb Open Data
datasources.auth: none, subject to StatsBomb attribution terms
datasources.coverage: Free event, lineup, match, and selected 360 data for covered competitions.
datasources.notes: Excellent for historical analytics and tactical/player profiles, not necessarily current World Cup live data.

datasources.name: Sportmonks Football API
datasources.coverage: Fixtures, livescores, events, teams, players, standings, odds, World Cup guides.
datasources.notes: Broad commercial football API; good candidate for a provider-specific adapter or row_claims manifests.

datasources.name: API-FOOTBALL
datasources.coverage: Fixtures, teams, players, standings, statistics, odds depending on plan.
datasources.notes: Likely useful if World Cup endpoints/plan coverage are available.

datasources.name: TheSportsDB
datasources.auth: free key or paid key depending endpoint/limits
datasources.coverage: Teams, players, events, artwork, highlights, basic sports metadata.
datasources.notes: Good enrichment source for display/media metadata; verify football coverage and rate limits per endpoint.

datasources.name: Open-Meteo
datasources.auth: none for common usage; observe license/limits
datasources.coverage: Venue weather forecasts and historical weather context.
datasources.notes: Optional edge/context only. Do not prioritize until core player, lineup, injury, tactical, and market modules are working.

x_social: "{team1}" "{team2}" (lineup OR injury OR squad OR training)
"{team1}" (injury OR lineup OR unavailable OR doubtful)
"{team2}" (injury OR lineup OR unavailable OR doubtful)
